strategic_capability_mapping.relationship: Makes parent-join continuation child handles stable across retry and polling when controlled continuation child tasks already exist after parent join materialization.
Lets unattended callers recover continuation child task ids from the existing task.run contract without raw ledger scraping or diagnostics-only RPCs.

questions.prompt: Does existing parent task.run output replay bounded child-orchestration handles after a consumed parent-join continuation has existing queued continuation children?
Is replay scoped to the latest consumed parent-join continuation and its accepted continuation handoff fingerprints?
Does repeated parent task.run avoid parent TaskRunning, parent join consumption, handoff envelope, child TaskRecord, and child TaskRunning mutation?
Do M5.31 initial replay, M5.30 first materialization, M5.29 replay-safe recovery budget behavior, in-budget recovery, and non-recovery task behavior remain intact?
Does this phase avoid raw child prompts, scheduler handoff, child auto-run, diagnostics RPCs, process exec expansion, network bypass, service control, patch apply, and workspace mutation paths?

answers.parent_join_child_handle_replay: Yes. TaskRunResult can replay child_orchestration_outcome for completed parent runs whose latest consumed parent-join continuation already materialized controlled continuation child TaskRecords that remain Queued.
answers.latest_parent_join_scope: Yes. The replay path locates the latest ParentJoinContinuationFingerprintConsumed admission id and returns queued child handles only for accepted continuation handoff fingerprints tied to that admission.
answers.no_replay_mutation: Yes. The replay path returns before task admission, so it appends no parent TaskRunning, parent join consumption, handoff envelope, child TaskRecord, or child TaskRunning event.
answers.prior_phase_compatibility: Yes. M5.31 initial parent replay still works before parent join consumption, M5.30 first materialization still returns the immediate outcome, M5.29 recovery_cycle_budget_outcome replay still wins without child handles, and explicit child task.run behavior remains unchanged.
answers.safety_boundary: Yes. The phase adds no raw child prompt exposure, scheduler handoff, child auto-run, external worker, process exec expansion, network bypass, service control, patch apply, workspace mutation path, diagnostics RPC, or wrapper-only blocked summary.

exit_criteria: Existing parent task.run output replays bounded child-orchestration handles for existing queued continuation children after a consumed parent-join continuation.
Parent-join child orchestration replay is scoped to the latest consumed parent-join continuation admission id and accepted continuation handoff fingerprints.
Repeated parent task.run after parent-join continuation child materialization appends no parent TaskRunning event.
Repeated parent task.run after parent-join continuation child materialization appends no parent join consumption or handoff envelope event.
Repeated parent task.run after parent-join continuation child materialization creates no child TaskRecord and no child TaskRunning event.
Existing parent inspection output remains consistent with the replayed child TaskRecord handles.
The replayed child-orchestration outcome reports materialized child ids/count and queued child ids/count.
The replayed child-orchestration outcome reports child_running_enabled=false and run_child_task_explicitly.
M5.31 initial parent replay behavior remains intact before parent-join continuation consumption.
M5.30 first materialization child-orchestration outcome behavior remains intact.
M5.29 replay-safe recovery-cycle budget outcome replay still returns without child-orchestration handles.
In-budget recovery-cycle child materialization and explicit task.run behavior still works.
Non-recovery tasks and initial controlled children preserve explicit task.run behavior.

No raw child prompts, provider responses, file contents, command strings, stdout, stderr, environment values, raw tool input objects, serialized request bodies, raw failure payloads, or unbounded error text are persisted or exposed.

No scheduler handoff, external worker, process exec expansion, network bypass, service control, patch apply, direct workspace mutation path, diagnostics wrapper RPC, child auto-run, or new blocked summary wrapper is added.

architecture_doc_tokens: replayed from existing queued continuation child
